item.title: Goldman Sachs Kicks Off Three-Part Investment-Grade Bond Sale
item.description: Goldman Sachs Group Inc. is tapping the US investment-grade debt market again, kicking off a new round of fundraising as banks self-fund following the release of first-quarter results.

item.title: Dutch Watchdog Warns of Private Credit ‘Canary in the Coal Mine’
item.description: The Netherlands’ financial markets regulator warned of further turmoil in the $1.8 trillion private credit market after turmoil at some direct lenders.

item.title: Drugmaker Leo Pharma Is Said to Pick Banks for Copenhagen IPO
item.description: Drugmaker Leo Pharma AS has lined up investment banks to lead a potential initial public offering as soon as this year in Copenhagen, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Polish Rates Seen on Hold if Iran Fallout Capped, Janczyk Says
item.description: Poland will likely keep interest rates unchanged in coming quarters as inflation is set to stay within the central bank’s tolerance range despite pressures from the Iran war, according to policymaker Wieslaw Janczyk.

item.title: Convenience Store Operator Yesway Seeks $321 Million in US IPO
item.description: Yesway Inc. is seeking to raise as much as $321 million in its initial public offering, joining a flurry of deals looking to finish despite the conflict in Iran.

item.title: Hungary Foreign Minister Shredding EU Documents, Magyar Says
item.description: Peter Magyar accused outgoing Hungarian Foreign Minister Peter Szijjarto of destroying documents related to European Union sanctions as he pledged to put an end to the country’s cozy ties with Russia.

item.title: CATL Is Said to Consider Up to $5 Billion Share Sale After Rally
item.description: Contemporary Amperex Technology Co. Ltd. is considering a share sale to raise up to $5 billion in Hong Kong following a stock rally, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Two Traders Convicted in Paris Over $23 Million Insider Coup
item.description: A Paris court convicted two men of insider trading in a secret network that made $23 million on a tip from a former Societe Generale SA banker about a confidential multibillion-dollar acquisition.

item.title: Hungary’s Magyar Calls for Swift Transition of Power After Vote
item.description: Peter Magyar called on Hungary’s president to ensure a swift transition of power after Prime Minister Viktor Orban’s party suffered a landslide election loss on Sunday.

item.title: Roblox Adds Youth Accounts as Push for Social Media Bans Grows
item.description: Roblox Corp. is for the first time introducing special accounts designed specifically for younger children and teens, as the company tries to head off the possibility of being swept up by various social media bans under consideration globally.

item.title: Brazil Inflation Forecasts Surge Above Target on Iran Fuel Shock
item.description: Brazil economists raised their 2026 inflation forecast above the central bank’s target ceiling, the latest fallout from the Iran war’s energy shock.

item.title: HSBC Said to Lose Second Dubai Bond Banker to Wall Street Rival
item.description: A senior Dubai-based bond banker at HSBC Holdings Plc is moving to Bank of America Corp., marking the second departure from the British lender in recent days as rivals continue to bolster staffing despite uncertainty stemming from the Iran war.

item.title: OPEC Output Suffers Record Plunge as Iran War Throttles Exports
item.description: OPEC crude production registered a record plunge last month as conflict in the Middle East throttled exports from key members, the group’s data showed.

item.title: Stock Market Trading Shows Fear of Risk Despite Weeks of Gains
item.description: On the surface, it appears that the stock market has been on a tear for the past couple of weeks. Look under the hood, however, and you’ll see that investors are buying but without the enthusiasm that usually marks the start of a major rally.

item.title: US Threat to Block Iranian Oil Faces Practical Obstacles
item.description: Plans to obstruct the waterway threaten to deepen the energy supply shock.

item.title: US Premarket Movers: Baker Hughes, Goldman, Leggett, Replimune
item.description: S&P 500 Index futures are down 0.7% as of 7:50 a.m. in New York after President Donald Trump ordered a blockade of the Strait of Hormuz following a deadlock in peace talks with Iran.

item.title: Orban and Aides Vanish From Sight After Hungarian Election Loss
item.description: Viktor Orban and his top lieutenants dominated Hungary’s airwaves over 16 years of his rule. But since last night’s landslide election, they are nowhere to be found.

item.title: India’s Shapoorji Seeks to Delay Paying $1.5 Billion of Debt
item.description: Shapoorji Pallonji Group, the Indian conglomerate behind the nation’s biggest-ever private credit deal, is trying to delay paying a separate debt, people familiar with the matter said, underscoring mounting strains amid volatile markets.

item.title: Ackman’s Joint Pershing Square, Fund IPO Begins Marketing
item.description: Bill Ackman has kicked off formal marketing for the US initial public offering of his closed-end fund and his hedge fund, even as the Iran war hits dealmaking optimism.

item.title: Harris’ 26North Raises $6 Billion for Debut Private Equity Fund
item.description: Josh Harris’ 26North Partners collected almost $6 billion for its debut private equity strategy, a US record for a first-time fundraising.

item.title: Goldman Bond Traders Miss Even as Stocks Team Beats Record
item.description: Goldman Sachs Group Inc.’s equity traders beat their own previous all-time quarterly high by more than $1 billion, as the war in Iran fueled market volatility, offsetting a disappointing haul by their fixed-income colleagues.

item.title: India Predicts Below-Normal Monsoon, Threatening Crop Production
item.description: India is likely to witness a below-average monsoon this year, adding pressure on farmers who are already facing higher input costs following the Middle East conflict.

item.title: Indonesia Hikes Nickel Ore Benchmark Price in Hit to Processors
item.description: Indonesia will hike benchmark prices for nickel ore, further weighing on local processors facing cost pressures from the Middle East war.

item.title: Musk Finds a Partner in Intel for His Huge Chipmaking Dream
item.description: The Tesla CEO is compiling a team for his planned Terafab chip manufacturing operation

item.title: Five Things to Watch at the IMF-World Bank Meetings This Week
item.description: For the fourth time in six years, the spring meetings take place at a moment fraught with global uncertainty.

item.title: Iran War Fuels Fears Over Global Economic Safety Net
item.description: ‘This is the worst place we’ve been in 20 years,’ crisis expert says

item.title: Adams Street Raises $7.5 Billion for Third Private Credit Fund
item.description: Adams Street Partners LLC has raised $7.5 billion for its third private credit vehicle focused on institutional investors, more than double the size of its previous fund, even as the market grapples with a retail exodus.

item.title: Holcim’s Lafarge Found Guilty Over Syria Terrorist Payments
item.description: Holcim Ltd.’s Lafarge was found guilty by a Paris court of making payments to terrorist organizations to keep open a cement plant during the civil war in Syria.

item.title: India’s Inflation Picks Up as Iran War Lifts Energy Costs
item.description: India’s inflation edged up in March as the war in the Middle East lifted crude prices and squeezed gas supplies for key industries.

item.title: ECB’s Vujcic Says Energy Prices Are Still Very Close to Baseline
item.description: European Central Bank Governing Council member Boris Vujcic said energy costs remain near the base-case scenario, despite rising since the recent cease-fire.
